item.title: A 2023 compilation: some new and mostly my ridiculous favorite ones

Hello Mr. Tumnus
It is only 5 days, but all the bread
Eternal favorite Paul Rudd ❤️
Gee who is this? Mads❤️
Ooooooooo my little boo thang Pedro ❤️
Heelys and SpongeBob 4EVA ❤️
Side eye from my OWN DOG jeez
FACTS
Cats are the best weirdos
:)
Animals are the best
Haha
Thank you kindly
I hope this is false
I hope this is true
American Airlines for me
brilliant
Please.
I would wear these, js
All the love with zoomies ❤️
Me.
Haha
We are all one.
It is the worst.
Smirks in office*
;)
Depeche Mode Baby
Smoothie yes
Wutang FOR KIDS
PERFECT. 2024 resolution
FACTS
Comment sections
Cornbread, Cornbread, Cornbread, cooooooRnnnnbReeaddddd
We miss y’all
Of them
2024 y’all !!!
*Art tax Tina Berning
Happy New Year everyone!

item.title: My wife died two years ago yesterday. I managed to make it all the way through the day without crying.
item.description: Then I watch the last episode of the new season of Futurama this morning and I can't stop. Front page edit.: Thank you all so much for your outpouring of empathy and support....the Imgur community is really like none other I have found.

item.title: Institutional conflicts of interest are normalized
item.dc:creator: CannonBallNuts

item.title: Travails of Women
item.description:
Gisella Perl was born in 1907 in Sighetu in Romania. She was the first Jewish graduate from the University Medical School in Cluj Napoca / Kolosvar, during which time she did some of her study in Berlin. She practiced as a gynecologist in Sighetu with her husband Dr Ephraim Krauss, and had a son & daughter. In 1944, as the previously anti-Semitic but nominally independent Romanian government began to seek a way out of the Axis Pact and make peace with the Allies before the country was defeated by the USSR, the Nazis took direct control over many areas of Romania, including Sighetu, and enacted their "Final Solution" policy of deporting Jews to concentration camps for forced labour and extermination. Dr Perl's daughter went missing during the process of deportation. Upon arrival at Auschwitz, Dr Perl - inmate number A25,404 - was seperated from her husband, son, and parents - her last memory of her father, Maurice, was his white-knuckled grip on a prayer book as he was led away, a gift she had purchased for him with her first fee as a Doctor. She was identified as a gynaecologist by Major Dr Victor Capesius (1907-1985), a former Romanian Army doctor who had transferred to the SS in 1943 as a Concentration Camp Pharmacist (KZ-Apotheker). Dr Perl and her husband had once hosted Dr Capesius for dinner in their home, both being graduates of the University Medical School in Cluj Napoca / Kolosvar. Dr Capesius brought Dr Perl to the Nazi Dr Josef Mengele (1911-1979), who was engaged in a series of human "experiments" of extreme brutality. This included the vivisection of pregnant women at various stages of pregnancy. Dr Perl was set the task of 'caring' for those who were set aside for Dr Mengele's experiments, with minimal equipment and resources - she was allowed one small knife, which was sharpened with rough stones found in the camp grounds.
[Picture: Dr Perl as a medical student.] The vast majority of Jewish women who arrived at the concentration camps detectably pregnant were gassed on arrival as unsuitable for forced labour, while those who managed to conceal their pregnancies were ultimately faced with a choice to either attempt an abortion, or allow the child to be born (which would require the risky co-operation of other prisoners to conceal) and then kill the baby; babies who were 'captured' by the Nazis were in some cases killed with lethal injections, but more commonly were drowned in buckets. Dr Perl was instructed directly by Dr Mengele that all pregnant prisoners were to be reported to him so he could select experimentation victims. At this point, she resolved that "there would be no pregnant women in Auschwitz". Dr Perl, who had strong professional and religious beliefs about abortion, made the difficult decision that to save women's lives, she must perform abortions. Without any medical instruments or anaesthesia, and often in the cramped and filthy bunks within the women’s barracks of Auschwitz, Perl ended the lives of countless foetuses, in hope that the mothers would survive and later, perhaps, be able to bear children. In some instances, the pregnancy was too far along to be able to perform an abortion. Perl would pierce the amniotic sac and manually dilate the mother’s cervix with her bare hands to induce labour. In these cases, the premature infants died, almost instantly. With the threat of a discovered pregnancy removed, the women were able to continue to work, gaining what for many was still only a temporary reprieve from their death sentences. Dr Perl was assisted in her work by Dr Lucie Adelsberger (1895-1971), a former immunologist (having been barred from practicing by Nazi law) German Jewish prisoner, and her secret night-time abortions took place in candlelight, held by Lea Fridler (b.1928, I believe she is still alive in 2023, Auschwitz number A25,402 - she was one person ahead of Dr Perl - A25,404 - in the queue at arrival at Auschwitz).
[Picture: Dr Josef Mengele, he escaped the collapse of Nazi Germany and died in 1979 after suffering a stroke while swimming in the sea off Bertioga, Sao Paulo, Brazil. His body was disinterred in 1985 and positively identified. He did not initially live "in hiding" in South America, he even requested - and received - a copy of his birth certificate from West Germany in 1956.] Most heart-rendingly, Dr Perl delivered the baby of a woman called Yolanda, who was also from Sighetu. Yolanda was then immediately sent into the medical ward of the camp with pneumonia, leaving the infant with Dr Perl. Initially, despite the many late-term abortions she had performed she found herself unable to kill the infant. But after two days, she was forced to act as the baby’s cries risked being heard. “I took the warm little body in my hands, kissed the smooth face ... then strangled him and buried his body under a mountain of corpses waiting to be cremated.” Amongst women prisoners, amenorrhoea (the ceasing of menstruation due to stress or physical deprivation) was very common, often causing additional mental horror to women who wondered that, even if they lived, they may never be able to have children. Amenorrhoea was not universal however; women were not provided with any kind of menstrual sanitary products, leading to a gendered black market of bundled rags which were jealously guarded. Menstruation not being a controllable biological process, many women suffered events like blood-dripping down their legs while being 'on parade' for roll call outside their barracks, which furthered the dehumanising effect of policies like head-shaving. Many survivors noted that continuing menstruation had the "advantage" of reducing sexual assaults from Guards, while Elisabeth Feldman De Jong (1916-2009), who suffered amenorrhoea, discovered that menstruating would dissuade Nazi doctors like Mengele from undergoing 'experiments' on women's reproductive organs, so borrowed the bloodied underwear of her menstruating sister to wear during inspections by doctors.
[Picture: Women assigned to slave labour, Auschwitz, May 1944.] Amidst the horror, one notable event of solidarity repeated in many sources is of women consoling and teaching teenagers who had their first periods in the camps, whose own mothers were already dead and so unable to teach them. By day, Perl assisted Mengele in his "research". He commanded her to deliver the first pair of twins born in Auschwitz, which she knew were destined for his infamous twin experiments. At one point he ordered the extraction of an eight-week-old foetus from a pregnant woman – intact – and preserved in a glass jar so it could be sent to Berlin. Mengele was deeply committed to the nonsense Nazi concept of "annihilating the biological basis of Jewry". Dr Perl was transferred to Bergen-Belsen camp as the Nazi situation on the Eastern Front fell apart & Soviet forces approached, where she found the body of her brother and sister-in-law in one of the many mounds of corpses in that camp - by this late stage the crematorium had stopped operating, and those massed at the camp lived alongside ever-growing amounts of bodies. She was at this camp on April 15th 1945 when she was liberated by British forces amidst a raging typhus epidemic.
[Picture: Dr Hughes stands before Belsen huts, which were burned by British flame-throwing armoured vehicles to cleanse the area of the typhus epidemic - amongst the officers also present was Richard Dimbleby (1913-1965), later famous as host of the BBC investigation show Panorama and all-nighter British General Election broadcasts; his 13 minute description of Belsen broadcast on the BBC on 19 April 1945 defined the Holocaust for a generation of Britons.] As British forces arrived, Dr Perl was delivering the baby of a Polish woman called Marusa, who began to hemorrhage, which was beyond the equipment Dr Perl had to stop - Dr Perl shouted at the British soldiers in French for help, and she was answered by Brigadier Dr Hugh Llewellyn Glyn Hughes CBE DSO MC MRCS (1892-1973) of the Royal Army Medical Corps, who rushed to her aid. Together, they stabilised Marusa and she gave birth to a healthy, free, baby. Dr Hughes would afterward direct the British military's attempt to save as many of the inmates as possible, aided by 100 recently-graduated students from London's various Medical Schools; several of these students had been preparing to work in India, and brought with them the crucial "Bengal Famine Mix", invented for the relief of Bengal's wartime famine of 1943, a gruel that was nourishing without overwhelming the wracked bodies of those who had long been starving.
[Picture: Dr Perl's book, which reads very much like an exorcism. It is notable that at no point in the narrative does she mention her still-missing daughter.] It is unclear how many babies were aborted or born & killed in the Nazi concentration camps; it is however notable that at liberation, other ethnicities and political prisoners were found to have some hundreds of babies amidst those liberated, while amongst Jews babies numbered in the dozens, which suggests that the Nazis took a special interest in the murder of their primary bogeyman's children. After spending 19 days moving from Displaced Person (DP) Camp to DP Camp, collecting and appealing for information about her family, Dr Perl was arrested by the Allies as a co-conspirator in Nazi crimes against humanity, but upon being established as an unwilling victim, she provided testimony at various trials of Nazis. She eventually established that her son (a talented violinist) and husband had both perished in the camps along with her parents and a greater part of her extended family. She attempted to kill herself and was hospitalised in a French convent-hospital until 1947, during which time she wrote her memoir of her time in Auschwitz.
[Picture: Perl, bottom left, the only female doctor on staff at the Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology at Mount Sinai Hospital in autumn 1966.] Dr Perl published her book in 1948 about her experiences in Auschwitz, which led to her meeting Eleanor Roosevelt (1884-1962), and it was with her support that Dr Perl became a US citizen in 1951 and opened a private obstetric practice in Manhattan. The majority of her patients were Holocaust survivors. She eventually became an infertility specialist at Mount Sinai Hospital, Manhattan; where she worked habitually with her sleeves rolled up and Auschwitz number on display. Dr Perl authored & co-authored nine academic papers that looked into treating diseases common to pregnancy. Several were co-authored with Dr Alan Guttmacher (1898-1974), a pioneer in policies to increase access to abortion and the contraceptive pill in the United States.
[Picture: A later re-issue of Dr Perl's 1948 book, showing her in her element. "I was" here feels much more like the past tense indicative than on her original cover.] She regularly lectured about her experiences in the Holocaust, and it was at one such event in 1978 she was informed that her daughter, Gabriella, who had been taken in by a non-Jewish family in 1944, had survived the war and now lived in Israel, leading to a joyous reunion and meeting her 28 year old grandson. In her post-war career, Dr Perl would deliver more than 3,000 babies. Dr Perl died in 1988 in Herzliya, Israel, where she lived with her daughter, she is buried in Sanhedria Cemetery, Jerusalem. When she and her husband had been seperated at Auschwitz in April 1944, his final words to her were "We will meet someday in Jerusalem." ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Richard Dimbleby's April 1945 BBC Broadcast on what he witnessed at Belsen Concentration Camp: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VP9BLKZENbc&t=145s ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ If you like my writing, and desire a much more uplifting tale, it may interest you to know my first book, about the Colonial Marines - enslaved African-Americans who joined British forces in the War of 1812, and gained their freedom - is now available for pre-order: UK Amazon: https://www.amazon.co.uk/Black-Redcoats-Colonial-Marines-1814-1816/dp/1399034014/ US Amazon: https://www.amazon.com/Black-Redcoats-Colonial-Marines-1814-1816/dp/1399034014/
item.dc:creator: BodicaRexVII

item.title: This is why Michigan rocks (plus it's pretty)
item.description:
Post FP Edit::: Imgurian @PerceptualEmergence reminded me (quite correctly) 4h ago: "You should always cite sources:" https://www.wzzm13.com/amp/article/news/politics/michigan-politics/new-michigan-laws-going-into-effect-2024/69-f2bf209d-9462-4958-85b8-07cef7a43d3a [ Please go to their comment and upvote them! https://imgur.com/gallery/709Qd3Z/comment/2370978456 ]
Gov. Whitmer and Lt. Gov. Gilchrist are both class acts. Michigan voters change their views every few years which makes us a "swing" state and I'm concerned about 2024 ... but right now having all 3 branches controlled by the Dems seems to be going in the right direction. And, to any GOP fear-mongers ... I can still have my gun ... no one is knocking down my local church ... and my kids have not been molested by swarms of gay people. GO FIGURE!!! Just a calibration moment. "For your consideration..." - Rod Serling Cheers!!
item.dc:creator: epicurusaquinas

item.title: Common brain rot thinking from a conservative
item.dc:creator: GravityVT

item.title: Better let you guys know it worked...
item.description:
I posted a few days ago, pretty sure I was ready to say my final goodbyes, but wanted to wait until after the holidays to do the deed, so I shouted into the Usersub void.
I did not want help. I did not ask for help. You loving jerks didn't care what I wanted or asked for. You cared about me.
It's weird to be learning this late in life what it feels like to be treated like a human. It's even more surreal to be learning from the messages in my glowing screens.
Thank you for changing my mind, for loving, for caring, for taking the time, for being you.
item.dc:creator: timetosaygoodbye40

item.title: Dump Actually
item.dc:creator: derek237

item.title: My maximalist apartment
item.description:
The living room, where about 75% of my books live. It's cluttered and I do need to move to a larger place (so I can acquire more stuff) but it's comfortable... I intentionally didn't clean up or anything so it's an accurate day-to-day look.
Other view of the living room
Entryway (front door to the right), with my collection of Victorian-era books on natural history and a little gallery wall of oil miniatures. The "bookcase" on the right is actually a vinyl sticker on my closet.
Natural history hallway, with nature on the right and human culture on the left.
Better angle of the nature wall
And the human wall, with bonus artwork from the bedroom.
item.dc:creator: overallpython

item.title: black cats are bad luck
item.dc:creator: Mebox9Player

item.title: I'm apparently a monster...
item.description: He's been spiraling down with dementia for the last 4 years, he's almost 89 and his health is failing terribly. My grandmother passed 10 years ago, and this summer he told me he just was happy he'd be able to see her again soon. Of course it makes me incredibly sad, and I will miss him all my life. But I also know and accept that death is just a natural part of life. I've cried openly at funerals of close friends my age. But feel a sense that life is normal when loved ones die at an old age. I get the impression my family feels like I am a monster because I don't have the same tearful reaction for both circumstances. Thanks for reading if you did. Don't care if this gets ignored or downvoted into oblivion. Just needed to get it off my chest. TLDR: Grandpa is dying, and my family thinks I'm terrible for grieving in my own way.
item.dc:creator: cameloDancer

item.title: Please Help
item.description: Hey folks, User timetosaygoodbye40 posted some troubling stuff about ending themselves after Christmas. They've not posted anything since. I'm not on Imgur that much so I can't figure out how to message them. If you can, please please please send some messages of support to them. Love you all Edit: I know it's a throwaway. I don't fucking care, I just want to maybe reach someone. I give zero fucks otherwise, I just want to help someone
item.dc:creator: jamaldo

item.title: My favourite meals from this year (that I made).
item.description:
Lobster and squash risotto.
Black pepper beef with prawn toast.
Loaded chilli potato wedges.
Karaage with gyoza and garlic-sesame broccoli.
Prosciutto wrapped cod with spinach, lentils and tomatoes.
Thai red curry with duck.
Chicken satay with gyoza.
Crab and ricotta tortellini with asparagus.
Steak, cheddar and jalapeño pie.
Prawn fried rice.
Prawn katsu curry.
Thai red curry with cod and king prawns.
Birria tacos. Hi folks, thanks for all of the love. I can't find all of the recipes off hand but if you want a particular recipe message me and I'll have a look for the correct one (or similar) and get back to you. Thanks for Front page :)
item.dc:creator: donttrythisathomeorelse

item.title: Someone handmade an impressive piece of "wacky" furniture inspired by Alice in Wonderland
item.description: Source: https://www.reddit.com/r/DIY/comments/18tx4lb/my_first_attempt_at_a_wacky_furniture_piece/
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: He's hiding, or there would be a picture
item.dc:creator: ToolPackinMama

item.description:
My yearly new years thing! I skipped 2022. I’m going to be very personal right now. My art accounts have always been a diary for me, but since I’ve been going through a lot of things I’ve been quiet. The amount of shitty people I’ve had to deal with this year is crazy. People who took advantage of me monetarily, emotionally, sexually etc. it really blows my mind how people can suck so hard, how people can really be terrible to you all while smiling in your face and pretending like they didn’t do anything wrong. I won't keep things negative though as i've cried more days this year than not, I'm not going to let anyone hurt me anymore as i go into 2024. I’m so grateful for all the amazing friends that I’ve retained in my life this year, I hope to keep all of them in my life and that we all have a great 2024. Especially my best friend you know who you are bro. All the people I grew close to in 2024 who have looked after me and kept me sane I love and appreciate you very much. My biggest thankyou is to every stranger who has ever complimented my outfit or makeup. You are so real for that!!! Thankyou as well to everyone here who supports my artwork and me. I really really appreciate all the love especially here on Imgur. I read every comment I get on this platform and appreciate every hat stays on comment. If you like my work consider supporting me on my patreon, miaormoa. Or my kofi, https://ko-fi.com/K3K0S2QNB
Old versions of my little new years tradition, I skipped my 2021 and 2022, but I actually started drawing these artworks at 17 years old.
item.dc:creator: miaormoa
